questionHtml:
Which structure represents a repeating unit of a polymer formed from propene?

A.  –CH2–CH(CH3)–

B.  –CH2–CH2–CH2–

C.  –CH(CH3)–CH(CH3)–

D.  –CH2–CH2–

meta.title: Which structure represents a repeating unit of a polymer formed from propene? A. –CH2–CH(CH3)– B. –CH2–CH2–CH2– C. –CH(CH3)–CH(CH3)– D. –CH2–CH2–
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 2. Models of bonding and structure
Structure 2.4.5—Addition polymers form by the breaking of a double bond in each monomer. Represent the repeating unit of an addition polymer from given monomer structures.
